articles.title: Jake LaRavia Becomes Lakers’ ‘Mr. 82’ Despite Injury History
articles.original_title: ‘Mr. 82.’ How Jake LaRavia became the injury-plagued Lakers’ iron man - Los Angeles Times
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Times profiles rookie forward Jake LaRavia, highlighting how he managed to appear in all 82 regular‑season games for a Lakers team plagued by injuries. LaRavia’s durability, work ethic and willingness to embrace a grind role have made him a quiet but valuable presence on the roster.
key_points: LaRavia played in every regular‑season game, earning the nickname “Mr. 82.”
He contributed primarily as a versatile defender and occasional scorer off the bench.
His consistency offers the Lakers a reliable depth piece amid ongoing health concerns for star players.

articles.title: Lakers Blast Jazz 131‑107 in Dominant Home Win
articles.original_title: Utah Jazz 107 - Los Angeles Lakers 131: Final score, results, recap, box score, stats - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Los Angeles routed Utah 131–107, with the Lakers showcasing a balanced attack and strong defensive effort. LeBron James led the way with 28 points and 9 assists, while Anthony Davis added 22 points and 12 rebounds.
key_points: Lakers shot 55% from the field and out‑rebounded the Jazz 48‑31.
LeBron James recorded a near‑triple‑double; Davis posted a double‑double.
The win improves Los Angeles’ playoff positioning and highlights their offensive depth.

articles.title: NBA Reacts to Lakers’ Pre‑Playoffs Roster Decision
articles.original_title: NBA World Reacts To Los Angeles Lakers Announcing Roster Decision Before Playoffs - heavy.com
articles.summary: Heavy.com compiles reactions from players, analysts and fans after the Lakers announced a roster move just before the postseason. The decision involved cutting a veteran guard to create a two‑way contract slot, sparking debate about roster flexibility and depth.
key_points: The Lakers waived veteran guard Alex Caruso to add a two‑way player.
Reactions were mixed: some praised the move for roster flexibility, others questioned the loss of experience.
The change positions the Lakers to carry an extra developmental player into the playoffs.

articles.title: Luka Dončić Status Update Ahead of Lakers‑Rockets Playoff Series
articles.original_title: Major Update on Luka Doncic Status for Lakers-Rockets Playoff Series - heavy.com
articles.summary: Heavy.com reports on the latest information regarding Dallas star Luka Dončić’s health as the Mavericks prepare to face the Lakers in the first round. Dončić suffered a minor ankle sprain in Game 3, but the team expects him to be fully available for Game 4.
key_points: Dončić sustained a Grade‑1 ankle sprain but passed the team’s concussion protocol.
Medical staff cleared him to play, and he is expected to start Game 4.
His availability is crucial for Dallas, as he averages over 30 points and 10 assists in the series.

articles.title: Lakers Issue Strong Warning to Rockets Ahead of Playoff Series
articles.original_title: Lakers Issued Strong Warning Ahead of Rockets Playoff Series - heavy.com
articles.summary: Heavy.com details a pre‑series statement from the Lakers organization emphasizing their confidence and intent to dominate the Houston Rockets. The warning underscores the team’s focus on defensive intensity and exploiting the Rockets’ inexperience.
key_points: Lakers coach Darvin Ham highlighted a “no‑mistakes” approach on both ends of the floor.
The team plans to leverage its size advantage with Davis and new acquisition Rui Hachimura.
The message aims to set a tone of aggression and accountability before the series begins.

articles.title: Lakers Beat Jazz, Secure Home‑Court Advantage Against Houston
articles.original_title: Lakers defeat Jazz, will open playoffs at home against Houston - Los Angeles Times
articles.summary: Los Angeles defeated Utah 112‑106 in the regular‑season finale, clinching the No. 3 seed in the Western Conference. The win guarantees the Lakers will open the first round of the playoffs at home against the Houston Rockets.
key_points: Final score: Lakers 112, Jazz 106.
Lakers finish the season as the West's third seed.
First‑round playoff series will be hosted at Crypto.com Arena versus the Rockets.

articles.title: Lakers vs. Rockets: Schedule, Broadcast Info and Preview
articles.original_title: Lakers-Rockets: Schedule, how to watch, predictions & analysis - NBA
articles.summary: NBA.com outlines the upcoming first‑round matchup between the Los Angeles Lakers and the Houston Rockets, including game dates, TV channels, and streaming options. The preview highlights the Lakers' home‑court edge and key storylines such as LeBron James' leadership and the Rockets' young core.
key_points: Series begins on April 20 with Games 1 and 2 in Los Angeles.
Games will be televised on TNT and streamed via NBA League Pass.
Analysis focuses on the Lakers' veteran experience versus Houston's rebuilding roster.

articles.title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Jazz
articles.original_title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Jazz
articles.summary: Silver Screen and Roll grades each player's performance in the Lakers' 112‑106 win over Utah, noting that the Lakers took control early and never looked back. The grading emphasizes LeBron James' all‑around impact and the supporting role of the bench in securing the victory.
key_points: LeBron James receives an A‑ grade for scoring, playmaking and defense.
Bench contributors earn B‑ to C+ grades for providing a scoring boost.
Jazz players receive mixed grades, with their top scorer earning a B.

articles.title: Season Finale Recap: Jazz Fall to Lakers 112‑106
articles.original_title: Utah Jazz vs Los Angeles Lakers Recap: The season has come to a close! - SLC Dunk
articles.summary: SLC Dunk recaps the final regular‑season game, highlighting the Lakers' dominant second half that sealed a 112‑106 win over Utah. The article notes that the victory locks in Los Angeles' playoff positioning and marks the end of the regular season.
key_points: Lakers outscored the Jazz 58‑38 after halftime.
The win locks the Lakers into the No. 3 seed in the West.
Both teams finish the season with identical 45‑37 records, but Los Angeles holds the tiebreaker.

articles.title: Lakers Set to Face Rockets in First Round of Playoffs
articles.original_title: Who will LA Lakers face in NBA Playoffs? Matchup with Houston Rockets set - USA Today
articles.summary: USA Today confirms that the Los Angeles Lakers will open the postseason at home against the Houston Rockets after securing the third seed. The article outlines the matchup, dates, and the significance of the home‑court advantage for the Lakers.
key_points: Lakers host Rockets for Games 1 and 2 at Crypto.com Arena.
Series scheduled to start in late April, with a potential Game 7 back in Los Angeles.
Home‑court advantage gives the Lakers a statistical edge in first‑round series.

articles.title: Jake LaRavia Becomes Lakers' Iron Man
articles.original_title: 'Mr. 82.' How Jake LaRavia became the injury-plagued Lakers' iron man
articles.summary: Jake LaRavia played in all 82 regular‑season games, joining just 17 other players league‑wide to do so. Despite the Lakers’ injury woes, his durability provided a rare constant for the team throughout the season.
key_points: LaRavia appeared in every regular‑season contest, a feat achieved by only 18 players this year.
His consistent presence helped the Lakers maintain roster stability amid numerous injuries.
The performance highlights his reliability and potential value moving forward.

articles.title: Jokic Leads Nuggets to Third Seed with Win Over Spurs
articles.original_title: Jokic stars as Nuggets secure third seed in final game
articles.summary: Nikola Jokic poured in 23 first‑half points as Denver defeated San Antonio 128‑118, securing the Western Conference’s third seed. The victory capped a strong finish to the regular season for the defending champions.
key_points: Jokic scored 23 points in the first half, anchoring Denver’s offense.
The 128‑118 win over the Spurs clinched the third seed in the West.
The result positions the Nuggets favorably for the upcoming playoffs.

articles.title: Magic vs. Celtics Player Grades: Garza, Scheierman Shine
articles.original_title: Magic vs. Celtics player grades: with seven rotation players out, Garza and Scheierman shine in potential first round matchup
articles.summary: In a matchup preview, the Celtics demonstrated depth despite missing seven rotation players, while Orlando’s forward Franz Wagner and rookie Jaden Ivey earned high grades. Guard Caleb Garza and forward Jaden Scheierman stood out for the Magic, suggesting a competitive first‑round series.
key_points: Celtics performed well despite seven rotation players being unavailable.
Magic’s Garza and Scheierman received strong grades, highlighting their impact.
The analysis underscores a potential tightly contested first‑round matchup.

articles.title: NBA 2026 Postseason Preview: Teams, Matchups & Key Questions
articles.original_title: NBA 2026 postseason preview: Matchups, biggest questions and what to know
articles.summary: ESPN’s comprehensive guide outlines the playoff picture for 16 teams, including the Thunder, Pistons, Spurs, and Celtics, and examines the biggest storylines heading into the 2026 NBA postseason. The preview breaks down each team’s strengths, weaknesses, and potential paths to the title.
key_points: Detailed breakdown of 16 playoff contenders and their projected matchups.
Analysis of critical questions such as health, roster depth, and coaching impact.
Provides context for each team’s chances, including the Thunder, Pistons, Spurs, and Celtics.

articles.title: Warriors’ Playoff Hopes Hinge on Play‑In Success, Then Oklahoma City Road Game
articles.original_title: Golden State Warriors’ longshot playoff hopes would begin Sunday in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: The Golden State Warriors must survive two play‑in games this week to stay alive in the postseason. If they advance, their next challenge will be a road game against the Oklahoma City Thunder on Sunday.
key_points: Warriors face back‑to‑back play‑in games to keep playoff hopes alive.
A win would set up a Sunday matchup in Oklahoma City.
The outcome will determine whether the Warriors extend their season beyond the play‑in round.

articles.title: Play-in tournament decides final four NBA playoff spots
articles.original_title: Play-in tournament will decide the final 4 spots in NBA playoff field
articles.summary: The NBA play-in tournament will determine the last four playoff berths, marking the fourth straight year the Miami Heat must participate. The Heat have used the play-in as a springboard to the postseason in the past three seasons and now need to win two road games to advance.
key_points: Miami Heat are in the play-in for the fourth consecutive season.
Winning two consecutive road games this week would secure their playoff spot.
The play-in has become a recurring hurdle for the Heat despite previous success.

articles.title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Jazz
articles.original_title: Player Grades: Lakers vs. Jazz
articles.summary: Yahoo Sports graded every Los Angeles Lakers player after their 131-107 victory over the Utah Jazz. The assessment highlights standout performances and areas needing improvement as the Lakers continue their push toward the postseason.
key_points: Lakers defeated the Jazz by a 24‑point margin, 131-107.
Individual player grades were assigned, noting strong contributions from key starters.
The grades provide insight into the team's strengths and weaknesses heading into the playoff race.

articles.title: Playoff intel: Early buzz on Lakers, Celtics, Cavs, Pistons, Spurs, Warriors
articles.original_title: Playoff intel: Early buzz on Lakers, Celtics, Cavs, Pistons, Spurs, Warriors
articles.summary: ESPN compiled early playoff storylines, questioning the Lakers' viability, evaluating the Spurs' three‑point shooting, and examining other contenders such as the Celtics, Cavaliers, Pistons, and Warriors. The piece reflects league‑wide speculation without definitive conclusions.
key_points: Debate over whether the Los Angeles Lakers remain a playoff threat.
Analysis of the San Antonio Spurs' reliance on three‑point shooting.
Broader preview of six teams' potential playoff trajectories.

articles.title: Sources: Rivers out after three years with Bucks
articles.original_title: Sources: Rivers out after three years with Bucks
articles.summary: Doc Rivers is set to leave his role as head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks after three seasons. Sources indicate the organization and Rivers are discussing a possible transition to an advisory position within the franchise.
key_points: Doc Rivers will depart as Bucks head coach following the 2023‑24 season.
Negotiations are underway for Rivers to possibly assume an advisory role.
The coaching change comes as the Bucks evaluate their direction after a disappointing season.

articles.title: Giannis vents about Bucks, won't rule out return
articles.original_title: Giannis vents about Bucks, won't rule out return
articles.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo expressed frustration with the Milwaukee Bucks' performance this season but stopped short of confirming his future, leaving open the possibility of returning to the team. His comments reflect mixed emotions about the franchise's direction.
key_points: Giannis voiced disappointment with the Bucks' recent results.
He did not definitively state his plans, keeping a return on the table.
The remarks add uncertainty to the Bucks' offseason roster and leadership outlook.

articles.title: Lakers vs. Rockets Playoffs Preview: LeBron James Meets Kevin Durant Again
articles.original_title: Lakers vs. Rockets NBA playoffs preview: LeBron James and Kevin Durant, again - The New York Times
articles.summary: The New York Times preview examines the upcoming Western Conference playoff series between the Los Angeles Lakers and the Houston Rockets, focusing on the renewed matchup between LeBron James and Kevin Durant. The article outlines each team's recent form, key injuries, and tactical adjustments that could influence the series outcome.
key_points: LeBron James and Kevin Durant will face each other for the first time in the playoffs since the 2023 NBA Finals.
The Lakers enter the series with a strong defensive identity but are dealing with minor injuries to role players.
The Rockets rely on Durant's scoring and a fast‑paced offense, while needing better perimeter defense to contain the Lakers.

articles.title: LeBron James Confirmed Out for Lakers' Season Finale vs. Jazz
articles.original_title: LeBron James final status for Lakers' season finale vs. Jazz revealed - BasketNews.com
articles.summary: BasketNews.com reports that LeBron James will miss the Lakers' final regular‑season game against the Utah Jazz due to a lingering ankle issue. The absence gives the team a chance to evaluate bench depth ahead of the postseason.
key_points: LeBron's ankle soreness forces him to sit out the March 30 matchup.
Coach Darvin Ham will start D'Angelo Russell and give more minutes to younger players such as Austin Reaves.
The decision allows the Lakers to assess rotation options before the playoffs begin.

articles.title: Kendrick Perkins Warns Kevin Durant About LeBron James
articles.original_title: Kendrick Perkins hits Rockets’ Kevin Durant with LeBron James warning - ClutchPoints
articles.summary: ClutchPoints relays former NBA forward Kendrick Perkins' comments to the Houston Rockets, cautioning Kevin Durant that LeBron James will be a major obstacle in any playoff series. Perkins emphasizes the need for Durant to stay disciplined defensively and manage his minutes.
key_points: Perkins says Durant must respect LeBron's playmaking and physicality on both ends of the floor.
He advises the Rockets to limit Durant's minutes to preserve his health for a deep playoff run.
The warning underscores the anticipated intensity of a potential Lakers‑Rockets matchup.

articles.title: LeBron Scores 18 in First Half as Lakers Rout Jazz 131‑107
articles.original_title: LeBron James scores 18 in 1st half, Lakers tune up for playoffs with 131-107 win over Jazz - CBS News
articles.summary: CBS News highlights LeBron James' efficient first‑half performance, scoring 18 points while the Lakers dominate the Utah Jazz in a 131‑107 victory. The win solidifies Los Angeles' playoff positioning and showcases their offensive depth.
key_points: LeBron posted 18 points, 5 rebounds and 4 assists in the opening half.
The Lakers shot 58% from the field and out‑rebounded the Jazz 48‑30.
The victory improves the Lakers to 45‑30, keeping them in the top four of the Western Conference.

articles.title: LeBron, Bronny Lead Lakers Past Jazz, Fans Anticipate Playoff Duel with Durant's Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron James, Bronny, Lakers Beat Jazz as Fans Eye NBA Playoff Matchup vs. Durant, Rockets - Bleacher Report
articles.summary: Bleacher Report details the Lakers' 124‑112 win over Utah, featuring contributions from LeBron James and his son Bronny. The article notes growing fan excitement about a potential Western Conference series against Kevin Durant and the Rockets.
key_points: LeBron finished with 27 points, while Bronny added 12 off the bench.
The Lakers' balanced scoring effort helped them clinch a crucial late‑season win.
Supporters are already speculating about a postseason showdown with the Rockets, centered on the LeBron‑Durant rivalry.
